audit_status.source_base: Greek Revelation text tradition with temple, witness, prophetic and Exodus/Elijah imagery; draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for measuring temple, two witnesses, beast from abyss, vindication, seventh trumpet.

verses.restored: A reed like measuring rod was given me, saying: Rise and measure temple of Divine and altar and those worshiping in it.
But the outer court of temple leave outside and do not measure it, because it was given to nations, and they will trample holy city forty-two months.
And I will give to my two witnesses, and they will prophesy one thousand two hundred sixty days, clothed in sackcloth.
These are the two olive trees and two lampstands standing before Lord of earth.
If anyone wills to harm them, fire goes from their mouth and consumes their enemies; if anyone wills to harm them, thus it is necessary for that one to be killed.

These have authority to shut heaven so rain may not fall during days of their prophecy, and authority over waters to turn them into blood/life and strike earth with every plague as often as they will.
When they complete their witness, the beast ascending from abyss will make war with them and conquer them and kill them.
Their corpse will be upon street of great city, spiritually called Sodom and Egypt, where also their Lord was crucified.
From peoples, tribes, tongues, and nations they look at their corpse three and a half days and do not allow their corpses to be placed in tomb.
Those dwelling on earth rejoice over them and make merry and send gifts, because these two prophets tormented those dwelling on earth.
After three and a half days, breath of life from Divine entered them, and they stood on their feet, and great fear fell on those seeing them.
They heard great voice from heaven saying, Come up here; and they went up into heaven in cloud, and their enemies watched them.
In that hour a great shaking happened, a tenth of city fell, seven thousand names of humans fell in the shaking, and the rest became afraid and gave glory to Divine of heaven.
The second woe passed; look, the third woe comes quickly.
The seventh messenger trumpeted, and great voices in heaven said: The reign of the world has become reign of our Lord and of his Messiah, and he will reign into ages of ages.
The twenty-four elders before Divine on their thrones fell on their faces and worshiped the Divine,
saying: We thank you, Lord Divine Almighty, the One who is and who was, because you have taken your great power and reigned.

The nations were enraged, and your wrath came, and season for dead to be judged, and to give reward to your servants the prophets and holy ones and those fearing your name, small and great, and to destroy those destroying earth.
The temple of Divine in heaven was opened, and ark of his covenant was seen in his temple; and flashes, voices, thunders, shaking, and great hail happened.

translation_notes: Two witnesses combine prophetic, temple, Moses/Elijah, and lampstand imagery; avoid identifying them with modern figures.
The beast appears first as abyssal imperial power opposing witness.
Destroying those destroying earth is a key ecological/anti-empire line.
safety_note: Witness imagery must not justify literal violence. Mouth-fire and plague signs are prophetic-symbolic judgment images within apocalyptic genre.
